rationale.R1.1: The artifact contains an explicit contribution claim ('deployment-validated automated turn-cue system') whose fidelity to the described study design is non-trivial to verify — the study instrumented teams with a 'meeting-analysis tool' and a 'structured protocol,' not obviously the same as an automated cue system. Reconstructing what is actually claimed vs. what was done is load-relevant under any plausible reading of 'take a look.'
rationale.R1.2: Material logical gaps are present regardless of reading: the HMM discourse-state analysis is introduced in methods but its output never appears in results; the conclusion that effect sizes 'match prior controlled-study findings' is hard to square with 23% (this study) vs. 32% (Wallace et al.). These internal inconsistencies are central under a content-review interpretation.
rationale.R1.3: The final contribution claim makes two specific quantitative assertions ('quantitatively improves satisfaction,' 'effect sizes match prior findings') that require checking against the reported data. The 23%-vs-32% mismatch and the gap between 'automated cue system' (claim) and 'structured protocol + instrumentation' (method) both hinge on evidence-claim alignment.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on whether the argument is internally consistent and the claims are faithfully supported by the described methods and results (logical/content review), or on improving the prose and writing quality (style/clarity review)?
